scope: FQ-09V: let one data id have multiple optional visual variants so terrain and items look less repetitive, selected deterministically from world seed + cell position, without save bloat or gameplay changes.
summary: BlockRegistry.visual_variant_textures(category, id): ordered pool from an explicit Array entry in visual_assets.json or the <id>_01.png consecutive-file convention (first gap ends the scan, MAX_VARIANTS 8); empty pool means the single-image visual_texture path is used exactly as before. visual_texture refactored onto a shared cached file loader; an explicit Array's first entry is the id's canonical single image, keeping HUD icons and enemy sprites coherent if an id gains a pool. world._build_tileset creates one TileSetAtlasSource per variant, each with identical collision/occlusion polygons, so variety can never change physics, lighting, or shelter; _source_ids became block_id -> Array of source ids; _set_tile picks posmod(hash(Vector3i(cell.x, cell.y, world_seed)), n) — same world, same look, nothing stored (an explicit empty-pool guard erases instead of indexing, per review). world.rebuild_tileset() rebuilds sources from art on disk and re-derives crack-overlay masks (smoke/dev hook; gameplay still loads art at world entry). Validator accepts path-or-array entries (all paths must exist; empty arrays fail); ASSET_TEMPLATE.md gained a Variants section; visual_assets.json documents the convention.
acceptance: A block id resolves several variant images when present: explicit array pool on dirt = 2, file-convention scan on a temp id = 2, pool-less stone = 0 (fq09v_variant_pools_resolve)

Selection is deterministic by seed/cell and never enters saves: two setups of seed 777 render identical variants across 40 sampled dirt cells with 2 variants in use (fq09v_deterministic_variant_selection); seed 778 changes the pick among shared cells, zero overlap/difference FAILS rather than passing vacuously (fq09v_seed_changes_selection)

A one-image asset works exactly as before and missing variants fall back cleanly: pool removal restores exactly 1 source and the generated dirt pixel (fq09v_fallback_after_removal); all fq07_* checks pass unchanged
Live world state restored before the FQ-08 section (fq09v_world_restored)
Smoke 190/190 PASS at 2026-07-09T16:15:47 (re-run green after review fixes) on waited windowed Godot 4.6.1; validator PASS; capsule doctor healthy; git diff --check clean
validation: python scripts/validate_repo.py -> PASS incl. array pool-entry rules
COHERONIA_SMOKE=1 waited windowed run -> 190/190 PASS incl. 5 new fq09v_* checks with self-cleaning temp art
decisions: Variants are extra tileset atlas sources selected at _set_tile time — the tileset is built once at _ready before any seed exists, so per-cell selection is the only placement that keeps determinism without rebuilds
Every variant of a block carries identical physics/occlusion polygons: variety is pixels only, provably unable to alter collision, lighting, or shelter math
The variant hash is hash(Vector3i(cell.x, cell.y, world_seed)) — pure function of seed+cell, regenerated on every load, so the save format is untouched by construction
An explicit array's first entry doubles as the single-image resolution for visual_texture consumers, so giving an id a pool never breaks the HUD or enemies
Blocks are the only variant renderer this pass; items/enemies accept pools in data but keep single images (deliberate scope hold)

Swarm pipeline: 1 haiku recon scout (pipeline map incl. the tileset-build-timing constraint), one implementation lane (orchestrator), sonnet diff review -> SIGNABLE; should-fix empty-pool guard + 2 clarity nits applied, 2 nits accepted as documented behavior; loop re-ran green
risks: The mining crack mask derives from the id's base texture, not per-variant pixels — variants should share one silhouette (documented; terrain blocks are full squares today)
world_seed truncates to 32 bits inside Vector3i for the variant hash — deterministic regardless; typical seeds are small
Runtime-broken pool paths are skipped (pool shrinks) rather than erroring; the validator enforces existence at repo level
The repo still ships zero art — the pipeline is proven via temp files; first real pool candidates are dirt/stone/grass
next_action: FQ-09A: future asset manifest and prompt packs.
